situation.title: Situation Cards
mat.title: Talking Mat
mat.tap_to_place: Tap emoji buttons to place items in zones
mat.credit: Inspired by Talking Mats™ framework — talkingmats.com
timer.title: Visual Timer
pecs.title: PECS Trainer
pecs.phase1_desc: Tap a picture to exchange it — learn to communicate a want
pecs.phase2_desc: Pick an item and bring it to the communication partner
pecs.phase3_desc: Choose the correct picture from multiple options
pecs.phase4_desc: Build 'I want' + item on the sentence strip
pecs.phase5_desc: Answer 'What do you want?' by building a sentence
pecs.bring_to_partner: Bring the picture to your partner
point.title: Point Talk
point.tap_words: Tap words to build a sentence
phrase.title: Phrase Builder
phrase.subject: Who
emotion.title: Emotion Map
emotion.where_feel: Where do you feel it in your body?
emotion.body.head: head
calm.title: Calm Room
calm.relax_instruction: Tense this area for 5 seconds, then relax
profile.choose_desc: Choose your profile or create a new one
profile.name: Name
calm.how_stressed: How stressed are you right now?
calm.advice_low: You seem calm. Great! Keep doing what you're doing. 😊
calm.advice_medium: Getting a bit tense. Try a short breathing exercise.
calm.advice_high: High stress. Take a break now. Try breathing or one of the strategies.
calm.advice_critical: Very high stress. Press the emergency button or go to breathing. You are safe. 💙
calm.emergency_msg: Take 5 deep breaths. Count each one. You are safe. Everything will be okay.
letter.title: Letter Journey
letter.mode.explore_desc: Tap letters to hear name, sound, and see mouth position
letter.mode.find_desc: Listen to a sound and find the right letter
letter.mode.soundout_desc: Break words into individual letter sounds
letter.mode.syllable_desc: Tap syllables to practice rhythm and stress
letter.speed_hint: Start slow — speed up as you improve (DTTC principle)
letter.explore_instruction: 🔤 Tap a letter to see and hear it!
letter.soundout_instruction: 📖 Sound out each letter in the word!
letter.syllable_instruction: 🥁 Tap each syllable — like clapping the rhythm!
letter.name: Name
letter.credit: Based on DTTC principles (Strand, 2020) for childhood apraxia of speech. Not a substitute for speech therapy.
letter.mouth.a: Open wide — like at the dentist
letter.mouth.b: Press lips together, then pop open
letter.mouth.c: Teeth together, tongue behind bottom teeth
letter.mouth.d: Tongue tip touches behind top teeth
letter.mouth.f: Top teeth on bottom lip, blow air
letter.mouth.g: Back of tongue touches soft palate
letter.mouth.h: Open mouth, breathe out warm air
letter.mouth.i: Big smile — lips wide, tongue high
letter.mouth.k: Back of tongue touches soft palate, release with a pop
letter.mouth.l: Tongue tip behind top teeth, voice on
letter.mouth.m: Press lips together, hum through nose
letter.mouth.n: Tongue behind top teeth, hum through nose
letter.mouth.p: Press lips together, then puff of air
letter.mouth.r: Tongue curls up — the trickiest sound!
letter.mouth.s: Teeth close, tongue behind bottom teeth, hiss like a snake
letter.mouth.t: Tongue tip taps behind top teeth quickly
letter.mouth.u: Lips pushed forward, small round opening
letter.mouth.v: Top teeth on bottom lip, voice on (like F but buzzing)
letter.mouth.aa: Round open mouth — like saying 'oh' but wider
letter.mouth.ae: Open mouth, tongue low — between A and E
letter.mouth.oe: Push lips forward, tongue high — like saying EE with round lips
letter.mode.coarticulation_desc: Practice smooth transitions between consonants and vowels — core motor planning
letter.coartic_instruction: 🗣️ Tap each combo to practice smooth sound transitions!
dict.title: Picture Dictionary
dict.pick_category: Pick a category to explore words!
dict.credit: Emoji-based picture dictionary for vocabulary building
focus.title: Focus Buddy
focus.work_done: Great focus session! Time for a break.
focus.break_done: Break's over! Ready to focus again?
focus.no_tasks_yet: No tasks yet — add one above!
focus.parked_desc: Got distracted? Park the thought here and come back to it later.
focus.credit: Pomodoro-based focus timer with parked thoughts for managing distractions
vs.title: Visual Support
vs.credit: Visual schedule and step-by-step support tool
break.title: Break Check
break.well_done: Well done! You took a great break!
break.credit: Guided break exercises based on sensory regulation techniques
break.step.feel_feet: Feel your feet on the ground
break.step.feel_legs: Notice your legs — are they tense?
break.step.feel_stomach: Feel your stomach — let it relax
break.step.feel_hands: Notice your hands — open them wide
break.step.look_5_see: Look around — name 5 things you can SEE
break.step.hear_3: Listen — name 3 things you can HEAR
break.step.touch_toes: Bend down and touch your toes
break.step.neck_circles: Gentle neck circles — slow and easy
break.step.feet_on_floor: Press your feet firmly on the floor
break.step.name_room: Name everything you see in the room
break.step.say_safe: Say: I am safe. I am here.
chooser.title: Chooser
chooser.instruction: Type your options and let me choose!
chooser.credit: Visual decision helper — great when choices feel overwhelming
energy.title: Energy Meter
energy.strat.1: Rest, cozy blanket, quiet music, drink water, ask for a hug
energy.strat.2: You're in a great zone! Keep doing what you're doing.
energy.strat.3: Perfect energy for learning and playing! Channel it into something fun.
energy.strat.4: Time to slow down. Try deep breaths, squeeze a stress ball, or take a short walk.
energy.strat.5: Stop and breathe. Find a quiet space. Count to 10. Ask an adult for help if needed.
diary.title: My Diary
diary.empty: No entries yet — tap + to write one!
diary.credit: Simple mood diary with emoji-based emotional tracking
guide.title: Step Guide
guide.pick: Choose a task to learn step by step!
guide.credit: Visual step-by-step task guides (TEACCH-inspired structured teaching)
guide.step.rub_20: Rub your hands together — count to 20
guide.step.dry: Dry your hands with a towel
guide.step.front_teeth: Brush your front teeth — up and down
guide.step.sweater: Put on a sweater if cold
guide.step.books: Put your books in the bag
counting.title: Counting
counting.credit: Visual counting tool with concrete objects
clock.title: Clock Teacher
clock.credit: Learn to read analog clocks with interactive practice
money.title: Money Check
money.tap_to_add: Tap coins and bills to count
money.credit: Learn Swedish money — count coins and bills
cook.title: Cooking Helper
cook.pick: What do you want to make?
cook.step.top_bread: Put the other bread on top
cook.step.banana: Peel a banana and break it in pieces
cook.step.add_pasta: Add pasta to the boiling water
clothes.title: Clothes Chooser
clothes.credit: Weather-based clothing picker for daily independence
practice.title: Practice Board
practice.credit: Repetition-based practice board for building daily skills
takk.title: TAKK Signs
takk.description: Signs as Alternative and Augmentative Communication (TAKK) — sign the key words while speaking
takk.sign.ja: Nod fist up and down (like knocking)
takk.sign.nej: Shake flat hand side to side
takk.sign.slut: Both hands open, push away from body
takk.sign.hjalp: Fist on open palm, lift upward
takk.sign.vill: Point forward with index finger, pull toward chest
takk.sign.inte: Flat hand, sweep sideways away from body
takk.sign.jag: Point to yourself with index finger
takk.sign.ata: Pinch fingers to thumb, move to mouth
takk.sign.dricka: C-hand shape, tip to mouth (like drinking)
takk.sign.lasa: Flat hands open like a book
takk.sign.glad: Hands circle upward on chest (happy feeling)
takk.sign.arg: Claw hand in front of face, tense expression
takk.sign.trott: Both hands on chest, drop downward
takk.sign.katt: Pinch and pull whiskers from cheeks
signDictionary.title: Sign Dictionary
signDictionary.intro: Learn signs from the Swedish Sign Language Dictionary
signDictionary.hint: Watch the video and try to copy the sign!
clothesChooser.title: Clothes Chooser
clothesChooser.part.head: What goes on your head?
clothesChooser.part.top: What do you wear on top?
clothesChooser.part.bottom: What do you wear on the bottom?
clothesChooser.readyToGo: You are ready to go outside!
practiceBoard.title: Practice Board
practiceBoard.intro: Tap a task to practice the steps!
socialStories.title: Social Stories
socialStories.school.1: It is morning. Time to get ready for school.
socialStories.school.2: I pack my bag with everything I need.
socialStories.school.3: I say hello to my teacher and friends.
socialStories.school.4: We learn new things in class. It is fun.
socialStories.school.5: At lunchtime, I eat my food and play.
socialStories.school.6: School is over. I go home. It was a good day!
socialStories.doctor.1: Today I am going to the doctor.
socialStories.doctor.3: I wait in the waiting room. I can read a book.
socialStories.doctor.4: The doctor is nice. They check that I am healthy.
socialStories.store.3: I help find things and put them in the cart.
socialStories.store.5: We go home with our bags. Good job!
socialStories.friend.1: My friend is coming to play!
socialStories.friend.3: I say hello and we decide what to play.
socialStories.friend.4: We take turns and share. That is kind.
socialStories.friend.5: We say goodbye. It was fun!
socialStories.haircut.1: Today I am getting a haircut.
socialStories.haircut.2: I sit in the chair. It is like a throne!
socialStories.haircut.3: The hairdresser cuts a little bit. It does not hurt.
socialStories.haircut.4: I look great! I did it!

memoryGame.title: Memory Game
angerManager.title: Anger Thermometer
rewardChart.title: Reward Chart
wordBuilder.title: Word Builder
sentenceBuilder.title: Sentence Builder
soundLibrary.title: Sound Library
pecsBoard.title: PECS Board
conversationAid.title: Conversation Aid
mySchedule.title: My Schedule
mySchedule.empty: No activities yet. Tap ➕ to add!
routineBuddy.title: Routine Buddy
timeTracker.title: Time Tracker
schoolDay.title: School Day
mathAid.title: Math Aid
about.title: About
about.tagline: Free apps for children with neurodevelopmental conditions, language disorders and intellectual disabilities
about.description: A free collection of evidence-based apps designed for children with neurodevelopmental conditions (NPF/NDD), intellectual disabilities (IF/ID), and speech-language difficulties. All apps work offline, are accessible, and use proven methods like AAC, TEACCH, PECS and LAMP.
about.audience.children: Children with NPF (autism, ADHD), intellectual disabilities, DLD, or verbal dyspraxia
about.audience.teachers: Special education teachers and school staff
about.feat.pwa: Works offline — install on any device
about.feat.i18n: Swedish and English (more languages coming via Transifex)
about.feat.a11y: Accessible: large touch targets, high contrast
about.feat.privacy: No tracking, no ads, no data collection
about.credit.signs: Swedish Sign Language (CC BY-NC-SA 4.0, Stockholm University)
welcome.talkBoard.purpose: A communication board (AAC) for children with neurodevelopmental conditions who need support to express themselves. Based on core vocabulary with LAMP principles.
welcome.talkBoard.how: Tap pictures to speak. Build sentences in the strip at the top. Buttons have fixed positions (LAMP) and Fitzgerald Key colors: yellow=people, green=verbs, orange=things, blue=descriptions.
welcome.talkBoard.goal: Give the child a voice and ability to communicate independently through picture and symbol support.
welcome.boardBuilder.purpose: Create custom AAC communication boards with ARASAAC pictograms.
welcome.boardBuilder.how: Choose size, search for pictograms, drag and drop to arrange. Save and use in daily life.
welcome.boardBuilder.goal: Give educators and parents tools to create individually adapted communication aids.
welcome.situationCards.purpose: Ready-made themed boards with pictures for common everyday situations.
welcome.situationCards.how: Choose a situation (food, play, school) and use the picture cards to communicate.
welcome.situationCards.goal: Facilitate communication in specific everyday situations through prepared visual supports.
welcome.talkingMat.purpose: Digital talking mat — a proven tool for expressing opinions by sorting pictures into zones.
welcome.talkingMat.how: Drag pictures to zones: green (like), yellow (neutral) or red (dislike).
welcome.talkingMat.goal: Give children the ability to express opinions and preferences, even with limited speech.
welcome.pecsTrainer.purpose: Practice picture exchange communication (PECS-inspired) — a structured way to learn communication with pictures.
welcome.pecsTrainer.how: The child selects a picture, gives it to a communication partner, and receives what the picture shows.
welcome.pecsTrainer.goal: Build basic communication skills step by step through picture exchange.
welcome.pointTalk.purpose: Aided language stimulation — children learn to communicate by pointing at pictures.
welcome.pointTalk.how: Show pictures and name them. The child points, you confirm and expand.
welcome.pointTalk.goal: Stimulate language development through structured pointing and naming.
welcome.phraseBuilder.purpose: Build sentences with pictograms — an AAC tool for expressing complex thoughts with pictures.
welcome.phraseBuilder.how: Select pictograms in the right order to build a sentence. Press play to hear the whole sentence.
welcome.phraseBuilder.goal: Develop the ability to express complex thoughts through symbol combinations.
welcome.pecsBoard.purpose: Communication boards with pictures sorted by needs, feelings and activities.
welcome.pecsBoard.how: Choose a category, tap pictures to communicate. Build sentence strips by combining pictures.
welcome.pecsBoard.goal: Quick and easy picture communication for everyday needs and wishes.
welcome.conversationAid.purpose: Ready-made phrases and support for conversations.
welcome.conversationAid.how: Choose a conversation situation, browse suggested phrases and tap to speak.
welcome.conversationAid.goal: Reduce anxiety around social conversations through prepared phrases and visual support.
welcome.socialStories.purpose: Visual social stories explaining everyday situations step by step.
welcome.socialStories.how: Read the story together with the child. Pictures and simple text explain what happens and how to act.
welcome.socialStories.goal: Prepare the child for social situations and reduce anxiety through understanding and predictability.
welcome.signDictionary.purpose: Learn sign language (TAKK) — sign dictionary with video.
welcome.signDictionary.how: Search for words, watch the video showing the sign, and practice together.
welcome.signDictionary.goal: Provide an additional communication method through visual signs complementing speech.
welcome.dayPlanner.purpose: Visual day planning with timeline. Creates structure and predictability.
welcome.dayPlanner.how: Add activities with pictures and times. Drag to arrange. Mark as complete during the day.
welcome.dayPlanner.goal: Create security through clear visual structure and predictability in everyday life.
welcome.pictureSchedule.purpose: Create visual daily schedules with pictures — basic TEACCH-inspired support.
welcome.pictureSchedule.how: Choose pictures for the day's activities, arrange top to bottom. The child checks off each step.
welcome.pictureSchedule.goal: Increase independence through visual structure the child can follow on their own.
welcome.mySchedule.purpose: Create a personal visual schedule adapted for the child.
welcome.mySchedule.how: Customize the schedule with the child's own activities, pictures and colors.
welcome.mySchedule.goal: A flexible schedule tool adaptable to each child's unique needs.
welcome.routineBuddy.purpose: Step-by-step routines for everyday — morning, evening, school.
welcome.routineBuddy.how: Choose a routine, follow the steps with pictures. Each step has a picture and brief instruction.
welcome.routineBuddy.goal: Build independence in daily routines through clear visual steps.
welcome.stepGuide.purpose: Break complex tasks into clear visual steps.
welcome.stepGuide.how: Create a guide with steps, pictures and short instructions. Follow one step at a time.
welcome.stepGuide.goal: Make difficult tasks manageable by breaking them into small, clear steps.
welcome.visualSupport.purpose: Visual schedules with step-by-step routines for common activities.
welcome.visualSupport.how: Choose a ready-made routine or create your own. Follow steps with pictures and text.
welcome.visualSupport.goal: Provide visual support that increases predictability and reduces the need for verbal instructions.
welcome.clothesChooser.purpose: Help choosing clothes based on weather and activity.
welcome.clothesChooser.how: The app shows the weather and suggests suitable clothes with pictures.
welcome.clothesChooser.goal: Increase independence when getting dressed through visual decision support.
welcome.cookingHelper.purpose: Visual cooking instructions step by step.
welcome.cookingHelper.how: Choose a recipe, follow steps with large pictures and short instructions. One step at a time.
welcome.cookingHelper.goal: Enable participation in cooking through clear visual instructions.
welcome.visualTimer.purpose: Visual countdown timer with first-then support.
welcome.visualTimer.how: Set a time, watch it decrease visually. First-then shows what happens next.
welcome.visualTimer.goal: Make abstract time concepts concrete and visual, reduce anxiety around transitions.
welcome.timeTracker.purpose: Track how long activities take with a visual timer.
welcome.timeTracker.how: Start the timer at the beginning of an activity, see time displayed visually.
welcome.timeTracker.goal: Increase time awareness and help with planning and transitions.
welcome.clockTeacher.purpose: Learn to read the clock with interactive exercises.
welcome.clockTeacher.how: Practice with analog and digital clocks. Drag the hands, answer questions and get feedback.
welcome.clockTeacher.goal: Build understanding of time concepts through playful interaction.
welcome.breakCheck.purpose: Reminders for regular breaks with activity suggestions.
welcome.breakCheck.how: The app reminds about breaks at regular intervals and suggests activities: movement, rest or play.
welcome.breakCheck.goal: Prevent overload through regular breaks and sensory balance.
welcome.practiceBoard.purpose: Track practice sessions and progress visually.
welcome.practiceBoard.how: Add what you're practicing, mark each time you practice. Watch your progress grow.
welcome.practiceBoard.goal: Make progress visible and build motivation through clear visual feedback.
welcome.schoolDay.how: Add lessons and breaks. See what's happening now and what's coming next.
welcome.schoolDay.goal: Reduce stress about the school day through clear visual structure and predictability.
welcome.emotionMap.purpose: Identify and express feelings with pictures.
welcome.emotionMap.how: Choose how you feel from the pictures. See where in your body you feel it.
welcome.emotionMap.goal: Develop emotional awareness and the ability to communicate about feelings.
welcome.calmRoom.purpose: Calming activities and sensory tools for winding down.
welcome.calmRoom.how: Choose from calming activities: deep breathing, soothing sounds, visual patterns.
welcome.calmRoom.goal: Provide tools for self-regulation and sensory balance in stressful situations.
welcome.focusBuddy.purpose: Help maintaining focus with timers and rewards.
welcome.focusBuddy.how: Set a focus period, work while the timer counts down. Get rewards for completed sessions.
welcome.focusBuddy.goal: Train focus ability with short, rewarding sessions that motivate.
welcome.energyMeter.purpose: Track and display energy levels during the day.
welcome.energyMeter.how: Mark your energy level during the day — high, medium or low. See patterns over time.
welcome.energyMeter.goal: Increase self-awareness about energy and rest needs for better self-regulation.
welcome.angerManager.how: Show how angry you are on the thermometer. Get strategy suggestions based on level.
welcome.angerManager.goal: Provide concrete tools to manage anger and prevent outbursts.
welcome.rewardChart.purpose: Earn stars for good habits and achieved goals.
welcome.rewardChart.how: Set goals, collect stars each time you achieve them. Reach the reward!
welcome.rewardChart.goal: Motivate through positive reinforcement and make progress toward goals visible.
welcome.diary.purpose: Visual diary with pictures, emojis and text.
welcome.diary.how: Write or choose pictures to tell about your day. Look back at old entries.
welcome.diary.goal: Provide a private space to express thoughts and feelings with optional support.
welcome.wordBuilder.purpose: Build words letter by letter with visual support.
welcome.wordBuilder.how: See a picture, hear the word, drag letters in the right order to spell it.
welcome.wordBuilder.goal: Develop literacy skills through playful, visual letter support.
welcome.sentenceBuilder.how: Choose words in the right order. Colors help: subject, verb, object. Hear the sentence read aloud.
welcome.sentenceBuilder.goal: Strengthen sentence building and grammatical understanding through visual cues.
welcome.letterJourney.purpose: Learn letters with phonics and animation.
welcome.letterJourney.how: See the letter, hear the sound, watch the animation. Practice forming the letter.
welcome.letterJourney.goal: Build phonemic awareness and letter knowledge as a foundation for reading.
welcome.pictureDictionary.purpose: Browse, learn and test your knowledge of everyday words with pictures.
welcome.pictureDictionary.how: Choose a category, see pictures with words. Test yourself by matching words and pictures.
welcome.pictureDictionary.goal: Build vocabulary through visual connection between picture and word.
welcome.mathAid.how: Count with pictures, drag objects and see results visually.
welcome.mathAid.goal: Make mathematics concrete and understandable through visual representations.
welcome.counting.how: Tap objects to count them. See the number grow. Hear the number read aloud.
welcome.counting.goal: Build basic number understanding by connecting quantity to numbers visually.
welcome.moneyCheck.purpose: Learn about money and giving change.
welcome.moneyCheck.how: Practice recognizing coins and bills, counting money and giving correct change.
welcome.moneyCheck.goal: Build practical money skills for increased independence in everyday life.
welcome.memoryGame.purpose: Classic memory game with pictures — train your memory in a fun way.
welcome.memoryGame.how: Flip cards, find matching pairs. Difficulty can be adjusted.
welcome.memoryGame.goal: Train working memory and concentration through play.
welcome.soundLibrary.purpose: Explore fun sounds sorted by category.
welcome.soundLibrary.how: Choose a category (animals, nature, instruments), tap to listen.
welcome.soundLibrary.goal: Stimulate auditory attention and the connection between sound and image.
welcome.chooser.how: Add alternatives with pictures or text. Spin the wheel or let the app choose.
welcome.chooser.goal: Reduce stress around choices by making the decision process visual and playful.
rewards.title: Rewards
rewards.no_stars_yet: No gold stars yet — keep practising and they'll come!
rewards.no_diplomas_yet: No diplomas yet. Unlock achievements to earn diplomas!
rewards.goal_reward_placeholder: What happens at the goal? (e.g. ice cream!)
rewards.diploma.title: Diploma
rewards.achievement.first_star.desc: You got your very first gold star!
rewards.achievement.five_stars.desc: You've collected five gold stars — fantastic!
rewards.achievement.ten_stars.desc: A whole ten gold stars — you're amazing!
rewards.achievement.twenty_five_stars.de: 25 gold stars! You're a real star!
rewards.achievement.five_minutes.desc: You've practised for five minutes — great start!
rewards.achievement.thirty_minutes.desc: Half an hour of practice — what an achievement!
app.oral_motor.desc: Practice oral motor movements for speech — tongue, lips, jaw and breathing
app.sound_chain.desc: Chain sounds together into syllables and words — visual chain links
app.speech_practice.desc: Structured speech training with the DTTC method — listen, imitate, practice
app.syllable_jump.desc: Jump through syllables — clap the rhythm and build words
welcome.oralMotor.purpose: Train the oral motor skills needed for clear speech. Adapted for children with neurodevelopmental conditions and childhood apraxia of speech.
welcome.oralMotor.how: Choose an exercise group (tongue, lips, jaw, breathing). Follow the steps — the app shows and tells you what to do.
welcome.oralMotor.goal: Strengthen oral motor control and movement planning as a foundation for better speech.
welcome.soundChain.purpose: Chain individual sounds into syllables and words. Support for children with neurodevelopmental conditions and childhood apraxia of speech (CAS).
welcome.soundChain.how: Start with individual sounds, tap to hear them. Chain more sounds together until you form whole words.
welcome.soundChain.goal: Build the ability to plan and execute sound sequences — the foundation for fluent speech.
welcome.speechPractice.purpose: Structured speech training inspired by the DTTC method. Support for children with neurodevelopmental conditions and childhood apraxia of speech.
welcome.speechPractice.how: Listen to the model, imitate simultaneously, try with a delay, and finally independently. Repeat many times!
welcome.speechPractice.goal: Build speech motor automatization through systematic practice in the right order.
welcome.syllableJump.purpose: Break words into syllables with visual and rhythmic cues. Support for children with neurodevelopmental conditions and childhood apraxia of speech.
welcome.syllableJump.how: See the word, tap each syllable in order. Clap along! Hear the whole word when all syllables are done.
welcome.syllableJump.goal: Develop syllable awareness and rhythmic speech planning.
oralMotor.title: Oral Motor
oralMotor.intro: Choose an exercise to train your oral motor skills!
oralMotor.tongue.desc: Move your tongue in different directions
oralMotor.tongue.up: Lift your tongue up to the roof of your mouth
oralMotor.tongue.down: Stretch your tongue down toward your chin
oralMotor.tongue.left: Move your tongue to the left
oralMotor.tongue.right: Move your tongue to the right
oralMotor.tongue.circle: Move your tongue in a circle
oralMotor.lips.puckerSmile: Pucker and smile, pucker and smile
oralMotor.jaw.openClose: Open and close, open and close
oralMotor.jaw.side: Move your jaw to the side
soundChain.title: Sound Chain
soundChain.intro: Chain sounds together into words! Start simple and build up.
speechPractice.title: Speech Practice
speechPractice.intro: Practice speech step by step with the DTTC method!
speechPractice.stageDesc.model: Listen to the model — hear how it should sound
speechPractice.stageDesc.simultaneous: Say it AT THE SAME TIME as the model
speechPractice.stageDesc.delayed: Wait a moment, then say it yourself
speechPractice.stageDesc.independent: Say it all by yourself without help!
syllableJump.title: Syllable Jump
syllableJump.intro: Break words into syllables — tap and clap along!
app.sentence_strip.desc: Build sentences with PECS sentence strips (Phase IV-VI)
app.symbol_text.desc: Type text and see pictograms above each word
app.comm_book.desc: Digital communication book with tabs, photos and pictograms
sentenceStrip.title: Sentence Strip Workshop
sentenceStrip.hint: Choose a starter phrase and add words to build a sentence
symbolText.title: Symbol-Supported Text
commBook.title: Communication Book
welcome.sentenceStrip.purpose: Sentence Strip Workshop helps children with autism build sentences step by step, based on PECS Phases IV-VI. The child selects a starter phrase like "I want" and adds words with pictograms.
welcome.sentenceStrip.how: 1. Select PECS phase (IV-VI). 2. Tap a starter phrase. 3. Choose a category and add words. 4. Tap 🔊 to hear the full sentence. 5. Save favorites!
welcome.sentenceStrip.goal: To develop sentence building and communication through visual support, aligned with the PECS methodology.
welcome.symbolText.purpose: Symbol Text automatically shows pictograms above each word you type — inspired by Widgit. Perfect as reading support for children with autism or intellectual disabilities.
welcome.symbolText.how: 1. Type a sentence in the text box. 2. Tap "Show with symbols". 3. Each word is shown with a symbol above it. 4. Tap 🔊 for speech.
welcome.symbolText.goal: To support reading comprehension and communication by linking text to visual symbols.
welcome.commBook.purpose: The Communication Book is a digital version of PECS communication books. With tabs for categories, personal photos and ARASAAC pictograms.
welcome.commBook.how: 1. Select a tab (food, drinks, etc.). 2. Tap a card to add it to the sentence strip. 3. Tap 🔊 to speak. 4. Use ✏️ to customize with your own photos and symbols.
welcome.commBook.goal: To give the child a personal, always-available communication book that can be customized with their own pictures and words.
app.speech_rhythm.desc: Prosody and rhythm training for CAS — tap along, stress patterns and intonation
app.sound_lottery.desc: Randomized phoneme practice — spin the wheel and practice sounds in isolation → syllable → word → sentence
app.mouth_gym.desc: Animated visual guides for tongue, lips and jaw — based on PROMPT therapy
app.word_parade.desc: Minimal pairs — words that differ by one sound. Critical phonological training
app.story_workshop.desc: Narrative speech practice — build simple stories with picture sequences and practice telling them
app.the_mimic.desc: Listen and repeat with increasing complexity — sounds → words → phrases → sentences. Core DTTC technique
speechRhythm.title: Speech Rhythm
speechRhythm.intro: Practice the rhythm and melody of speech!
speechRhythm.tap.instruction: Tap the drum in time with the syllables!
speechRhythm.intonation.instruction: Follow the melody curve with your voice!
welcome.speechRhythm.purpose: Prosody and rhythm training for children with CAS. Practices stress, tempo and intonation.
welcome.speechRhythm.how: Listen to the rhythm, tap along, and practice stress patterns. A visual metronome helps you keep time.
welcome.speechRhythm.goal: Improve the natural rhythm and melody of speech — a key skill in CAS therapy.
soundLottery.title: Sound Lottery
soundLottery.intro: Spin the wheel and practice sounds!
welcome.soundLottery.purpose: Randomized phoneme practice with hierarchical progression — from isolated sounds to full sentences.
welcome.soundLottery.how: Spin the wheel to get a sound. Practice it in isolation, then in syllables, words and sentences.
welcome.soundLottery.goal: Systematic practice of all Swedish sounds with motivating randomization.
mouthGym.title: Mouth Gym
welcome.mouthGym.purpose: Visually guided oral motor exercises based on PROMPT therapy principles.
welcome.mouthGym.how: Choose a muscle group, follow the animation and do the exercises in front of a mirror.
welcome.mouthGym.goal: Strengthen oral motor control — the foundation for clear speech.
wordParade.title: Word Parade
wordParade.intro: Hear the difference between similar words!
welcome.wordParade.purpose: Minimal pair training — words that differ by just one sound. Critical for phonological awareness.
welcome.wordParade.how: Listen to word pairs, practice saying them, and guess which word is playing.
welcome.wordParade.goal: Improve the ability to hear and produce sound differences between similar words.
storyWorkshop.title: Story Workshop
storyWorkshop.intro: Build a story and tell it!
welcome.storyWorkshop.purpose: Narrative speech practice for DTTC's 'connected speech' phase. Build stories with pictures.
welcome.storyWorkshop.how: Choose pictures, arrange them into a story, listen to the model and practice telling it yourself.
welcome.storyWorkshop.goal: Develop connected speech — from single words to complete narratives.
theMimic.title: The Mimic
theMimic.intro: Listen and mimic — from simple to complex!
welcome.theMimic.purpose: Core DTTC technique — listen and repeat with increasing complexity.
welcome.theMimic.how: Listen to the model, mimic it. Start with simple sounds and work up to full sentences.
welcome.theMimic.goal: Build motor speech planning through systematic imitation.
app.sagostunden.desc: Create fun stories by choosing words and pictures
sagostunden.pick_words_hint: Tap a word to add it, or skip to get a random one!
sagostunden.no_saved: No saved stories yet. Create one!
welcome.sagostunden.purpose: Create fun stories by picking words and pictures.
welcome.sagostunden.how: Choose a story template, pick words with pictograms, and a unique story is generated for you!
welcome.sagostunden.goal: Practice reading and creativity through visual storytelling.
sit.head: head
story.morning.title: Good morning!
story.morning.scene2: The alarm rings. Time to wake up!
story.morning.scene5: The child is ready for the day!
story.park.title: At the park
story.park.scene1: The child walks to the park.
story.rain.title: It's raining!
story.birthday.title: Party!
story.birthday.scene4: The child blows out the candles.
story.pet.title: The Cat
story.pet.scene1: The cat sits in the window.
talk.no_boards: No boards yet. Create one in Board Builder!
welcome.restTrainer.purpose: Practice smooth transitions between syllables — helps children with verbal dyspraxia (CAS) improve prosody, rhythm, and timing.
welcome.restTrainer.how: Listen to the model, then try to repeat with the same rhythm and stress. Rate your attempt with stars.
welcome.restTrainer.goal: Smooth, rhythmic speech with correct stress patterns and natural syllable transitions.
restTrainer.title: Syllable Transitions
restTrainer.level1.name: Real syllables (2)
restTrainer.level2.name: Nonsense syllables (2)
restTrainer.level3.name: Three syllables
restTrainer.level4.name: Variable stress
restTrainer.level5.name: Speed challenge
app.prosody_trainer.desc: Practice speech melody, stress, and rhythm
welcome.prosodyTrainer.purpose: Practice speech melody, stress, and rhythm! Helps children with verbal dyspraxia make their speech more expressive.
welcome.prosodyTrainer.how: Choose a level and practice stress in words, question and statement melodies, and emotional expression.
welcome.prosodyTrainer.goal: Improve the natural melody and rhythm of speech.
prosody.title: Prosody Training
prosody.question: Question
prosody.level1.title: Stress in two-syllable words
prosody.level2.title: Stress in three-syllable words
prosody.level3.title: Question or statement?
prosody.level4.title: Emotional expression
prosody.level5.title: Full sentences
app.melody_therapy.desc: Melodic Intonation Therapy — sing to learn to speak
welcome.melodyTherapy.purpose: Melodic Intonation Therapy helps children with verbal dyspraxia find words through singing.
welcome.melodyTherapy.how: Choose a phrase and practice in four steps: hum, sing, speak-sing, and finally speak normally.
welcome.melodyTherapy.goal: Build bridges from song to speech — step by step.
melodyTherapy.title: Melody Therapy
app.mouth_motor.desc: Visual articulation guide — see WHERE to place tongue, lips and jaw for each Swedish sound
welcome.mouthMotor.purpose: See exactly how to shape your mouth for each sound. Helps children with verbal dyspraxia (CAS) understand where to place their tongue, lips, and jaw.
welcome.mouthMotor.how: Tap a sound to see a cross-section of the mouth and a front view of the lips. Listen to the sound and see an example word with a picture.
welcome.mouthMotor.goal: Build awareness of mouth positions as a complement to DTTC therapy.
mouthMotor.title: Mouth Motor
mouthMotor.intro: Tap a sound to see how to shape your mouth 👄
tongueViewer.title: Tongue Viewer
tongueViewer.purpose: Tongue Viewer is a visual biofeedback simulator showing animated tongue and mouth positions for Swedish speech sounds. It displays a sagittal cross-section (side view) of the mouth with smooth animations showing exactly where the tongue should be for each sound — like having an ultrasound view of the mouth, but animated and interactive.
tongueViewer.how: Choose a sound category (vowels, stops, fricatives, etc.) and tap a sound to see the animated tongue position. The SVG cross-section shows the tongue, palate, teeth, lips, and airflow in real time. Use the ghost overlay to show a target position, compare two sounds side by side, or play practice sequences that animate through common Swedish syllable patterns with audio.
tongueViewer.goal: Help children with speech and language disorders understand and visualize correct tongue positions for Swedish sounds. By seeing exactly where the tongue should be, children develop kinesthetic awareness that supports speech therapy. The progressive difficulty levels (vowels → stops → fricatives → liquids → retroflexes) follow standard articulation therapy progression.
tongueViewer.comparison.title: Compare Sounds
welcome.promptGuide.purpose: The PROMPT guide shows where and how to touch the child's face to support correct oral-motor movements for each sound.
welcome.promptGuide.how: Select a sound → see the face diagram with touch points → follow the step-by-step guide with pressure, direction, and timing.
welcome.promptGuide.goal: Give parents and therapists a visual guide for PROMPT technique in speech therapy.
promptGuide.intro: Choose a sound category to begin. Each sound shows where and how to touch the child's face.
promptGuide.safety.title: Important Information
promptGuide.safety.consultFirst: This is a guide — always consult a speech therapist before using PROMPT technique.
promptGuide.safety.gentlePressure: Always use gentle pressure. Touch should be soft and supportive.
promptGuide.safety.stopIfUncomfortable: Stop immediately if the child shows discomfort or resistance.
promptGuide.safety.reminderShort: Always consult a therapist · Use gentle pressure · Stop if uncomfortable
promptGuide.videoDesc: Instructional video for this sound is planned.
prosody.intro: Choose a level and practice speech melody, stress, and rhythm!
prosody.level1.desc: Practice which syllable gets the emphasis in two-syllable words
prosody.level2.desc: Find the stressed syllable in longer words
prosody.level3.desc: Listen and decide — is it a question or a statement?
prosody.level4.desc: Express emotions through your voice — happy, sad, angry, surprised
prosody.level5.desc: Put it all together with complete sentences
prosody.rhythm.title: Rhythm Training
prosody.rhythm.desc: Practice the rhythm and beat of speech with a metronome
prosody.stress.instruction: Which syllable is stressed? Tap the correct one!
prosody.intonation.instruction: Listen and choose — is it a statement, question, or exclamation?
prosody.emotion.instruction: Listen to the emotion and try to match it!
prosody.emotion.hint.glad: Happy — voice goes up, bright and light
prosody.emotion.hint.ledsen: Sad — voice goes down, slow and soft
prosody.emotion.hint.arg: Angry — voice is loud, sharp and fast
prosody.emotion.hint.förvånad: Surprised — voice jumps up high suddenly
prosody.fullSentence.instruction: Listen and repeat with the same melody and rhythm!
app.perspective_taking.desc: Understand other people's thoughts, feelings, and perspectives
app.sequence_trainer.desc: Arrange events in the correct order
app.category_sort.desc: Sort pictures into the right categories
app.sound_sensitivity.desc: Manage sound sensitivity — log triggers, learn coping strategies, and practice gradual exposure
app.sensory_profile.desc: Map sensory needs and build a sensory diet
sound.welcome.title: Sound Sensitivity
sound.welcome.purpose: This app helps you manage sound sensitivity. You can log which sounds are difficult, learn coping strategies, and practice listening to sounds at low volume.
sound.welcome.how: Use the diary to track which sounds bother you, try the coping strategy cards, and use the exposure trainer to gradually get comfortable with sounds.
sound.welcome.goal: To feel more in control when encountering difficult sounds.
sound.distress: How much does it bother you?
sound.diary.title: My Sound Diary
sound.diary.empty: No sounds logged yet. Tap a sound to rate it.
sound.strategy.tap_to_learn: Tap a card to learn more
sound.strategy.tip.ear_defenders: Put on ear defenders or ear plugs to reduce loud sounds. Keep a pair in your bag!
sound.strategy.tip.deep_breathing: Breathe in slowly for 4 seconds, hold for 4, breathe out for 4. This calms your body.
sound.strategy.tip.count_to_ten: Close your eyes and slowly count to 10. Focus on each number.
sound.strategy.tip.pressure_blanket: Wrap yourself in a heavy blanket or ask for a firm hug. Deep pressure helps calm the nervous system.
sound.strategy.tip.quiet_place: Go to a quiet place where there are fewer sounds. It's okay to take a break!
sound.strategy.tip.music_headphones: Put on headphones and listen to music you like. It can block out other sounds.
sound.exposure.title: Sound Exposure Trainer
sound.exposure.instructions: Start with low volume. Try to listen a little longer each time!
sound.traffic.title: How do I feel?
sound.traffic.message.green: I'm doing okay with the sounds right now.
sound.traffic.message.yellow: The sounds are hard for me right now. I might need a break soon.
sound.traffic.message.red: I need help! The sounds are too much for me right now.
sound.map.title: My Sound Map
sound.map.tap_place: Tap a place to rate it
categorySort.title: Category Sort
categorySort.tapToHear: Tap picture to hear the name
categorySort.dragHint: Drag the picture to the right box
welcome.categorySort.purpose: Practice sorting pictures into the right category. Trains classification and vocabulary!
welcome.categorySort.how: Drag each picture to the right box. You hear the name when you tap a picture.
welcome.categorySort.goal: Sort all pictures correctly and earn stars!
sensory.title: Sensory Profile
sensory.welcome.purpose: Map sensory needs and create a personal sensory diet with strategies for everyday life.
sensory.welcome.how: Rate how each sense responds — seeking, neutral, or avoiding. Get a visual profile and personalized strategies.
sensory.welcome.goal: Understand sensory preferences and build a daily plan with helpful activities.
sensory.profiles.name: Profile name
sensory.profiles.noProfiles: No profiles yet. Create one to start!
sensory.assessment.instructions: For each item, choose how the person responds:
sensory.chart.title: Sensory Profile Chart
sensory.chart.noData: Complete the assessment to see your sensory profile chart.
sensory.strategies.title: Recommended Strategies
sensory.strategies.noData: Complete the assessment to get personalized strategies.
sensory.strategy.touch.seek.2: Play with clay, slime or sand
sensory.strategy.touch.seek.3: Deep pressure hugs or weighted blanket
sensory.strategy.smell.seek.3: Use essential oils or scented stickers
sensory.diet.title: Sensory Diet
sensory.diet.instructions: Drag activities into time slots to build a daily sensory diet.
sensory.diet.noActivities: No activities added yet. Drag from the list!
sensory.export.title: Export Profile
welcome.perspectiveTaking.purpose: Learn to understand what others think and feel.
welcome.perspectiveTaking.how: Read stories and answer questions about how people feel, what they think, and what you would do.
welcome.perspectiveTaking.goal: Develop the ability to take other people's perspectives and understand their thoughts and feelings.
pt.intro: Choose a level to practice understanding how others think and feel!
pt.question: Question
pt.level1.label: Simple Emotions
pt.level2.label: Complex Emotions
pt.level3.label: False Beliefs
pt.level4.label: Advanced Thinking
pt.level4.desc: What does A think B thinks?
pt.think.love_it: "I love this! This is exactly what I wanted!"
pt.think.want_new: "I want a new one! That was my ice cream!"
pt.think.want_fix: "I want someone to fix my toy!"
pt.think.everyone_laughs: "Everyone is watching and laughing at me!"
pt.think.want_same: "I want the same thing! It's not fair!"
pt.think.friend_mad: "My friend will be mad at me."
pt.do.help_away: Help them get away from the dog
pt.do.ask_play_together: Ask if you can play together
pt.l1.present.scenario: Erik gets a present. He opens it. It's a toy he has wished for a long time!
pt.l1.ice_cream.scenario: Sara drops her ice cream on the ground. It falls in the dirt.
pt.l1.dog.scenario: A big dog runs towards Lisa in the park. Lisa has never seen such a big dog before.
pt.l1.toy_broken.scenario: Oscar's favorite toy car breaks. A wheel falls off and he can't fix it.
pt.l1.birthday.scenario: It's Emma's birthday! All her friends come with presents and cake.
pt.l1.friend_crying.scenario: Lisa sees that her friend is crying during recess at school.
pt.l1.friend_crying.think_q: What does Lisa think about her friend?
pt.l2.stage.scenario: Axel is performing on stage. He forgets the words and everyone is watching.
pt.l2.drawing.scenario: Alma worked really hard on a drawing and the teacher puts it up on the wall for everyone to see.
pt.l2.new_toy.scenario: Your friend gets a brand new toy that you really wanted. They show it to you.
pt.l2.new_toy.do_q: What would be a good thing to do?
pt.l2.race.scenario: Elias practised running every day for the race. But he came in last place.
pt.l2.secret.scenario: You accidentally told someone your friend's secret. Now your friend found out.
pt.l2.secret.do_q: What would be the best thing to do?
pt.l2.surprise_party.scenario: When Alice comes home, everyone jumps out and shouts 'Surprise!' It's a surprise party!
pt.l3.ball.step1: Sara puts her ball in the basket.
pt.l3.ball.step3: While Sara is outside, Anna moves the ball to the box.
pt.l3.ball.step4: Sara comes back inside. She wants her ball.
pt.l3.ball.question: Where does Sara THINK the ball is?
pt.l3.ball.explanation: Sara thinks the ball is in the basket because that's where she left it. She didn't see Anna move it! Sara doesn't know what happened while she was outside.
pt.l3.choc.step1: Mum puts the chocolate in the cupboard.
pt.l3.choc.step3: While Mum is away, Dad moves the chocolate to the drawer.
pt.l3.choc.step4: Mum comes home and wants the chocolate.
pt.l3.choc.question: Where does Mum THINK the chocolate is?
pt.l3.choc.explanation: Mum thinks the chocolate is still in the cupboard because she put it there. She didn't see Dad move it!
pt.l3.teddy.step1: Erik puts his teddy bear under the pillow.
pt.l3.teddy.step2: Erik goes to brush his teeth.
pt.l3.teddy.step3: Little brother takes the teddy and puts it in the wardrobe.
pt.l3.teddy.step4: Erik comes back and wants his teddy.
pt.l3.teddy.question: Where does Erik THINK the teddy is?
pt.l3.teddy.explanation: Erik thinks his teddy is still under the pillow because that's where he left it. He didn't see his little brother move it!
pt.l3.cookie.step1: Lisa puts the cookies on the table.
pt.l3.cookie.step3: Dad moves the cookies to the jar.
pt.l3.cookie.question: Where does Lisa THINK the cookies are?
pt.l3.cookie.explanation: Lisa thinks the cookies are on the table because that's where she left them. She was outside and didn't see Dad move them!
pt.l3.book.step1: Alma puts her book on the sofa.
pt.l3.book.step3: Big brother moves the book to the shelf.
pt.l3.book.step4: Alma comes back to the living room.
pt.l3.book.question: Where does Alma THINK her book is?
pt.l3.book.explanation: Alma thinks her book is on the sofa because she put it there. She didn't see big brother move it to the shelf!
pt.l4.surprise.step1: Anna secretly buys a present for Erik at the toy shop.
pt.l4.surprise.step2: Erik sees Anna through the shop window, but Anna doesn't notice him.
pt.l4.surprise.step3: Anna doesn't know that Erik saw her.
pt.l4.surprise.q1: What does Anna THINK Erik knows?
pt.l4.surprise.a1_nothing: Nothing — Anna thinks it's still a secret
pt.l4.surprise.a1_knows: Anna thinks Erik knows about the present
pt.l4.surprise.q2: Will Erik be surprised when he gets the present?
pt.l4.surprise.a2_no: No, because he already saw her buying it
pt.l4.surprise.explanation: Anna thinks Erik doesn't know about the present because she didn't see him looking through the window. But Erik already knows! So he won't be truly surprised, even though Anna thinks he will be.
pt.l4.ice_cream.step1: The ice cream truck is in the park. Both Lisa and Olle know this.
pt.l4.ice_cream.step2: Lisa goes home. The ice cream truck drives to the town square.
pt.l4.ice_cream.step3: Olle sees the ice cream truck drive to the square.
pt.l4.ice_cream.step4: Lisa doesn't know the truck moved.
pt.l4.ice_cream.q1: Where does Lisa think the ice cream truck is?
pt.l4.ice_cream.q2: Where does Olle think Lisa will look for the ice cream truck?
pt.l4.ice_cream.explanation: Lisa thinks the truck is still in the park because she didn't see it move. Olle knows the truck moved, but he also knows that Lisa doesn't know. So Olle thinks Lisa will look for it in the park!
pt.l4.hat.step1: Maja puts her hat in her backpack.
pt.l4.hat.step2: Axel sees Maja put the hat there.
pt.l4.hat.step3: Axel moves the hat to the coat pocket when Maja isn't looking.
pt.l4.hat.step4: Maja comes back to get her hat.
pt.l4.hat.q1: Where does Maja think her hat is?
pt.l4.hat.q2: Where does Axel think Maja will look?
pt.l4.hat.explanation: Maja thinks her hat is in the backpack because that's where she put it. Axel knows he moved it, but he also knows Maja didn't see him. So Axel knows Maja will look in the backpack first!
pt.l4.cake.step1: Mum secretly bakes a cake for Alice's birthday.
pt.l4.cake.step3: Alice asks Dad: "Do you know if Mum is planning anything?"
pt.l4.cake.step4: Dad says: "No, I don't know anything."
pt.l4.cake.q1: Does Dad know about the cake?
pt.l4.cake.q2: Why does Dad say he doesn't know?
pt.l4.cake.a2_surprise: To keep the surprise for Alice
pt.l4.cake.explanation: Dad really does know about the cake, but he lies to keep it a surprise for Alice. Sometimes people say things that aren't true to be kind — like keeping a surprise party secret!
pt.l4.rain.step1: It's raining outside. Viktor has an umbrella in the closet.
pt.l4.rain.step2: Elsa sees the rain and thinks about Viktor.
pt.l4.rain.step3: Elsa doesn't know that Viktor already took his umbrella.
pt.l4.rain.q1: What does Elsa think Viktor needs?
pt.l4.rain.a2_no: No, he already has his umbrella
pt.l4.rain.explanation: Elsa thinks Viktor needs an umbrella because she doesn't know he already took it. Viktor is fine! Elsa's thinking is kind, but based on incomplete information.
app.time_perception.desc: Understand abstract time concepts through visual activities
app.planning_trainer.desc: Executive function training — break down tasks, estimate time and prioritize
app.fine_motor.desc: Fine motor skills training — finger exercises, tracing and pattern copying
tp.title: Time Perception
tp.duration.title: How long is...?
tp.duration.desc: Choose an activity to see how long it takes. A timer will run while showing the activity.
tp.estimation.title: Time Estimation Game
tp.estimation.desc: Can you feel how long time is? Press stop when you think the right amount of time has passed!
tp.estimation.hint: Press STOP when you think {seconds} seconds have passed...
tp.sequence.title: Before, After, Now
tp.sequence.desc: Put the cards in the right order. Tap a card, then tap a slot to place it.
tp.vocab.title: Time Words
tp.vocab.desc: Learn words about time. Tap the card to see what the word means.
tp.comparison.title: What Takes Longer?
tp.comparison.desc: Compare two activities. Which one takes more time?
tp.comparison.question: What takes longer?
tp.day.title: My Day
tp.day.desc: A visual timeline of a typical day. Tap an activity to hear about it.
welcome.timePerception.purpose: Helps children understand abstract time concepts like duration, sequence, and time vocabulary.
welcome.timePerception.how: Six activities: watch how long things take, guess time, sort events in order, learn time words, compare durations, and explore a day timeline.
welcome.timePerception.goal: Make invisible time concepts concrete and visual, building intuitive understanding of duration and sequence.
welcome.planningTrainer.purpose: Training for executive functions — helps older children and teens break down big tasks into smaller steps, estimate how long things take, and prioritize what to do first.
welcome.planningTrainer.how: Choose a task or template. Break it into steps with pictures. Guess how long each step takes. Sort by priority. Then check off steps as you go!
welcome.planningTrainer.goal: Build skills for planning, time awareness and self-organization — key executive functions for independence.
plan.title: Planning Trainer
plan.breakdownIntro: Let's break this task into smaller steps!
plan.breakdownInstruction: Break the task into 3 to 6 smaller steps. Tap the plus to add a step.
plan.timeIntro: How long do you think each step takes?
plan.timeInstruction: Guess how many minutes each step will take. Compare with the suggested time!
plan.priorityIntro: How important and urgent is this task?
plan.priorityInstruction: Choose how important and urgent this task is. Tap the box that fits best.
plan.checklistIntro: Time to do the work! Check off each step as you finish it.
plan.reviewIntro: How did it go? Let's reflect!
plan.reviewInstruction: Think about how it went. Rate your work and think about what you can do better next time.
plan.whatCanImprove: What can be better next time?
plan.reviewDone: Great job! Your plan is saved.
plan.tpl.packBag.s4: Close the bag and put it by the door
plan.credit: Executive function training — task planning with visual support
fineMotor.title: Fine Motor Skills
welcome.fineMotor.purpose: Train fine motor skills with fun finger exercises — tapping, tracing, pattern copying and more.
welcome.fineMotor.how: Choose an exercise and follow the instructions. Start easy and difficulty increases as you improve.
welcome.fineMotor.goal: Improve finger coordination, precision, and hand-eye coordination through playful practice.
fineMotor.tapping.title: Finger Tapping
fineMotor.tapping.desc: Tap the circles that appear as fast as you can! Speed increases over time.
fineMotor.tracing.title: Tracing Paths
fineMotor.tracing.desc: Trace along the dotted path with your finger. Try to stay on the line!
fineMotor.tracing.instruction: Draw along the dotted line with your finger
fineMotor.pattern.title: Pattern Copying
fineMotor.pattern.desc: Watch the pattern, then tap to reproduce it! Grid grows as you level up.
fineMotor.connect.title: Connect the Dots
fineMotor.connect.desc: Tap the numbered dots in order to reveal the picture!
fineMotor.pinch.title: Pinch & Spread
fineMotor.pinch.desc: Use two fingers to zoom in or out on the image to match the target size.
fineMotor.pinch.hint: Use two fingers to pinch/spread, or scroll wheel on desktop
fineMotor.drawing.title: Drawing Challenges
fineMotor.drawing.desc: Draw the shapes shown! Pick colors and brush sizes.

welcome.purpose: Learn important traffic safety rules and road skills.
welcome.how: Tap on the activities to practice safe walking and crossing streets.
welcome.goal: Become confident and safe around traffic and roads.

activities.traffic-lights-desc: Learn when to stop, wait, and go
activities.vehicles-desc: Learn about cars, buses, and other vehicles

traffic-lights.title: Traffic Lights
traffic-lights.tap-light: Tap the light to change it

safe-places.title: Safe Places
safe-places.drag-to-sort: Drag items to SAFE or DANGEROUS zones

reflectors.title: Reflectors & Visibility
reflectors.can-you-see: Can you see in the dark?

app.traffic_safety.desc: Learn road safety with traffic lights, signs, and vehicles
